name: Vall del Bac and Llongarriu Loop via Vall de Bianya
name_original: Vall del Bac & Llongarriu - Vuelta circular por el Valle de Bianya
description: This difficult 30.4-mile touring cycling route through Vall del Bac and Llongarriu in the Valley of Bianya offers challenging climbs and sce
region_intro: Hiking around La Vall De Bianya offers diverse natural landscapes within the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park, featuring extinct volcanoes and a "valley of valleys" terrain. The region is characterized by lush forests of holm oak, beech, and oak, alongside expansive meadows and flowing streams. Mountains like Puig Ou provide panoramic views of the surrounding terrain. An extensive network of trails caters to various hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around La Vall de Bianya The most popular hiking route is Hostal de la Vall de Bianya loop from Sant Salvador de Bianya, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 26 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers varied terrain and views of the local landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sant Miquel del Mont Church – Sant Andreu del Coll loop from l'Hostalnou de Bianya, a moderate 7.4 miles (11.9 km) path. This trail connects historical Romanesque churches and provides scenic valley vistas. Local hikers also love the House loop from Sant Joan les Fonts, a 3.7 miles (6.0 km) trail leading through local hamlets and woodlands, often completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes. Hiking around La Vall De Bianya is defined by volcanic landscapes, extensive valleys, and dense forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging treks. The routes in La Vall De Bianya are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 1000 reviews. More than 8000 hikers have used komoot to explore La Vall De Bianya's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in La Vall De Bianya?
A: La Vall De Bianya offers a wide selection of routes, with over 18 hiking trails available. These range from easy strolls to more challenging treks, ensuring options for various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain and landscapes can I expect while hiking in La Vall De Bianya?
A: The region is known for its diverse natural landscapes, including the unique volcanic terrain of the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park. You'll encounter lush forests of holm oak, beech, and oak, expansive meadows, and flowing streams. The area is characterized by a 'valley of valleys' structure, offering varied scenery and panoramic views from peaks like Puig Ou.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in La Vall De Bianya?
A: Yes, La Vall De Bianya has several easy trails. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38199805" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;House loop from Sant Joan les Fonts&lt;/a&gt; is a gentle 3.7-mile (6.0 km) path through hamlets and woodlands, typically completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes. There are 4 easy routes in total.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I discover along the hiking routes?
A: La Vall De Bianya is rich in history. You can explore sections of the ancient Capsacosta Roman Road, which features informative panels. The region also boasts approximately fifteen Romanesque churches scattered throughout the valleys, offering historical insight and scenic beauty. Many routes pass by traditional farmhouses and even modernist buildings.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more demanding experience, there are 2 difficult routes available. These trails often lead to higher elevations, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20078874" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montmajor Summit – Toralles loop from la Vall del Bac&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 12 km with significant elevation gain, offering rewarding views.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in La Vall De Bianya?
A: Many of the trails in La Vall De Bianya are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38187779" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hostal de la Vall de Bianya loop from Sant Salvador de Bianya&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.4 miles (10.3 km) long and takes around 3 hours 26 minutes.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see while hiking in the area?
A: Yes, the region features several beautiful waterfalls. You can visit highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/435265" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg de Malatosca Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3304941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Cotilla Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. These natural features add to the scenic beauty of the trails.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in La Vall De Bianya?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 1200 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from volcanic zones to lush forests, and the well-maintained network of trails that cater to all abilities. The blend of natural beauty and historical sites like Romanesque churches is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints or peaks to reach for panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. Routes in La Vall De Bianya lead to prominent peaks like Puig Ou (1,300 m) and Puigsacreu, which offer splendid panoramic vistas of the surrounding terrain and even the distant Canigó massif in the Eastern Pyrenees. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4300607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wolf Peak&lt;/a&gt; is another popular summit for views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in La Vall De Bianya?
A: La Vall De Bianya is enjoyable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors, making them ideal. Summer can be warm, but the lush forests provide shade. Winter hikes are also possible, especially on lower elevation trails, offering a different, serene experience.

Q: Are there any trails that connect historical Romanesque churches?
A: Yes, several routes allow you to explore the region's rich religious heritage. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31884858" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel del Mont Church – Sant Andreu del Coll loop from l'Hostalnou de Bianya&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7.4-mile (11.9 km) path that connects these historical sites and provides scenic valley vistas.

Q: Is public transport available to access hiking trailheads in La Vall De Bianya?
A: While specific public transport options directly to every trailhead may be limited due to the rural nature of the valley, major towns in the Garrotxa region often have bus connections. It's advisable to check local bus schedules in advance or consider driving to access the starting points of the trails.
tips: A good place to have lunch

The small village of Castellfollit de la Roca stands on a spectacular basaltic cliff, 50 meters high and almost a kilometer long, formed by the flow of the Fluvià river. The oldest part of the village dates back to the Middle Ages and is made up of squares, streets and shaded corners that offer relief from the summer heat. The narrow alleys of the village lead to the old church of Sant Salvador which dates back to the 13th century. From here you can enjoy an unparalleled panorama of the lush valley below: Castellfollit is in fact located close to the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Nature Reserve.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is classified as a difficult touring bicycle route, best suited for experienced cyclists. It features a significant elevation gain of nearly 960 meters over its 49 km distance, including a long and hard climb in Vall del Bac with steep and sustained gradients that require good fitness.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a varied terrain, including national roads, cycle paths, and streets, suggesting a mix of paved and potentially unpaved sections. You'll encounter a long asphalt road ascent in Vall del Bac, which includes hard ramps and a 'treacherous layout' of climbs and descents. Some sections in the broader region are known for uneven roads, potholes, and even gravel.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect beautiful and varied scenery, characteristic of the Garrotxa region. The Vall de Bianya offers a rural and bucolic landscape with centuries-old farmhouses, churches, hermitages, pastures, crops, and forests. Views often include the majestic Pyrenees, and the area is known for its lush green vegetation.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or places to stop on the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by several interesting points. You can find refreshments at Hostal de la Vall de Bianya, admire the religious architecture of Mare de Déu dels Àngels de Llongarriu, and pass through the unique settlement of Castellfollit de la Roca.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact route start are not detailed, the route is circular and located in the Valley of Bianya. It's advisable to look for public parking in one of the villages within the Valley of Bianya, such as Hostalnou de Bianya, which offers convenient access to the local road network.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Garrotxa region, including the Valley of Bianya, is known for its lush green vegetation due to consistent rainfall. While specific seasonal advice isn't provided, spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for cycling. Summers can be hot, and winters might be cooler, especially at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other trails. Notably, it covers 43% of the Ruta Transpirinenca en BTT (Cap de Creus - Campodron), 23% of [CIMA GI08] Capsacosta, and 17% of El Camí IT1TR3 El Camí Capçaler: de Salzes a Balaguer. You'll also find smaller sections overlapping with the Volta a peu a la Garrotxa and Sant Pau - Resclusanys - Vall del bac.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for touring bicycles, and while dogs are generally welcome in the Garrotxa region, bringing a dog on a challenging 49 km bike tour of this difficulty might not be suitable for all pets. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it is accustomed to long distances and varied terrain, and always keep it on a leash, especially near roads or in populated areas.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle in the Parc Natural de la Zona Volcànica de la Garrotxa?
faqs.answer: The route is located within the Parc Natural de la Zona Volcànica de la Garrotxa. Generally, there are no entrance fees or specific permits required for cycling on public roads and marked trails within the natural park. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of the natural environment.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: On average, this touring bicycle route takes approximately 3 hours and 30 minutes to complete. However, this duration can vary significantly based on your fitness level, the number and length of stops you make, and the specific conditions on the day of your ride.

faqs.question: Is this a circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the name suggests, this is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends at the same point, allowing you to return to your starting location without needing to retrace your steps or arrange for transport.
